text: A persistent misconception treats Attention Restoration Theory as arguing that nature is passively restful — that the absence of stimulation heals the fatigued mind. The Kaplans explicitly rejected this reading. Directed attention fails not from too much input but from too much inhibitory demand: constantly suppressing irrelevant information wears out the inhibitory mechanism. What natural environments provide is not silence but the right kind of signal — one whose moderate unpredictability rewards attention without demanding that alternatives be suppressed. A bird's irregular call doesn't require you to ignore a competing bird's call. The ecology manages salience so the brain doesn't have to.
question: According to Attention Restoration Theory, what actually causes directed attention to fatigue?

options.text: Overload of sensory input overwhelming the brain's processing capacity
Excessive inhibitory demand from constantly suppressing irrelevant information
Absence of novel stimulation causing the attention system to idle and weaken
High emotional arousal that prevents the prefrontal cortex from sustaining focus
